MODULE 4	Brief Example - Biz Opp
Brief
EXAMPLE
Biz Opp
(00:02)
I want to go through another brief example with you. This one is going to be for
a more biz-op-oriented product. This is a brief that I did, back in 2014, 2015,
for the Lion’s Publishing guys for a Biz Opp kind of type of offer, how to make
money online without having skills or training or things like that. I actually
came up with this product through research as well, and I don’t think it really
ever saw the light of day, but I actually thought it was a pretty interesting
product. Remember, obviously, this stuff is getting done from-- populated by
the research we’ve done as well as through our work with the mechanism and
figuring out a unique mechanism for it.
(00:49)
So, for this offer, which is, again, a Biz Opp type offer, who’s the audience?
Well, generally, the lower income, straddling the poverty line, 45-plus believe
that, just like young people are making millions on the internet, or even billions,
they could too. Maybe they’ve had some business ideas in the past that their
friends have said are good, but they either don’t have the money to act, or
have never acted. Others may just be semi-retired or retired and looking for
something productive to do with their time.
(01:20)
What are the pain points or biggest fears? Identify the one to three huge ones,
while the rest are secondary. So, in the short term, can’t pay the bills, tired
of living on food stamps or being unemployed, low educational background
means shitty jobs in fast food, physical labor, etcetera, tired of that, tired of
that life, tired all the time. If they are employed, likely working multiple jobs just
to make ends meet, tired of living in neighborhoods where their kids’ bikes get
stolen from the front yard. Ones who are closer to middle class are worried
about retirement, worried about having enough money to last them the next
several decades, especially with kids, grandkids, spouses. Even those with
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 16 --

4	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 4	Brief Example - Biz Opp
one single decent job are tired of working for someone else, of the burden of
9 to 5, of having to constantly struggle with this stuff.
(02:02)
So, this is worth noting because in this case of Biz Opp, it’s not going after
necessarily like young dudes or bros who want to own a marketing agency.
This one’s going after a colder, broader traffic of people who are kind of
lower-income and looking for a better way in their lives, and it’s important to
differentiate that. But then we still want to talk about people who are in that
bracket who are more middle-class, or have a decent job, and why would they
be interested in this as well, or what is their pain point. Longer term, being
poor for the rest of their lives and never having enough money for their kids,
loved ones, etcetera, struggling and working until they die, or living off food
stamps until they die, driving shitty cars, having bad credit, being stuck with
loans they can’t pay off.
(02:45)
For middle-class, being stuck in a job they hate for the rest of their life, not
having enough for retirement, dying and never having done so many of the
things they dreamed of doing. See, as I mentioned in another one of these
videos, we get more existential towards the end of the longer term stuff. And
a lot of it is, basically, everyone has a bucket list, and dying before you get to
check a lot of those things off your bucket list because you didn’t have the
money to do them, that’s like a long-term pain point. But it’s a very real pain
point that people have, and a very real fear.
(03:14)
So, by reversing the pain points and biggest fears, what is the one big promise,
or I guess a couple, you’re going to make, how this will change their lives
forever? I said a couple ‘cause of the 1-3. So, we’ll show them a simple plug
and play business opportunity that they can use directly online, requires no
cash to get started, and no business experience. If you have an email address
and the internet, you can do it. Maximizes the concept of leverage, one where
you exploit a unique opportunity by putting yourself in a powerful situation.
Probably won’t make them hundreds of millions of dollars because that’s
unrealistic, but a couple hundred thousand dollars from this over time, at least
$5,000 per month, enough to pay off bills, get out of debt, get new cars-- or
probably a new car versus new cars, it’s a little difference-- new car, clothes
for their kids, all the things they wish they could do and have--I’ll just put do--
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 16 --

5	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 4	Brief Example - Biz Opp
but haven’t because of money.
(04:11)
All right. What solutions for the reader’s problems exist already, and why
aren’t they working for them? Well, you know you can make it rich some
other ways, spend hundreds of thousands of dollars to try to get a college
and postgraduate degree, work in a job for the next 40 years, and try to work
your way up to $100,000 a year, maybe even $200,000 per year. But generally,
these people are at a point in their life where that’s not going to happen.
(04:37)
And that’s actually important ‘cause that’s what they all didn’t believe. I’ve
been in Ubers before where I’m talking to the driver and telling them how I do
marketing. And this has happened multiple, multiple times, they’re like, “Oh,
so is that what your degree’s in?” And I say, “No,” and they’re shocked. And
then they tell me about how, “I want to do marketing or business, but I don’t
have a degree,” or, “I didn’t finish college, so nothing I can do now.” And you’re
like, “Okay. Cool. A ton of multimillionaires never went to college.”
(05:08)
But there’s a belief about that, right? And so, that’s how they believe that this
solution isn’t a real solution for them and doesn’t work. They also think they
could start another business, maybe a website, or an app, or something. But
do they have experience in that? Do they want to spend tens of thousands of
dollars and more hiring programmers? They probably don’t have that kind of
money or the confidence to dive into something like that. They could stay in
their current jobs, but they hate those jobs already. And then there are other
Biz Opp courses out there, but most of the market for this product hasn’t been
exposed to them. And even if they have, they probably found out that it was
a lot of promises and not a lot of substance. They felt ripped off, or cheated,
made them feel even more hopeless.
(06:05)
So, what’s the new claim, root cause, surprising info, the mechanism here?
Well, there are millions of products sitting on the shelves of stores in America
right now that simply will never sell at their main retail price. What happens
to them? The retailer or the manufacturer continues to cut the price on these
products until, eventually, they becomes so cheap that someone will buy
them. Usually, these products end up being sold in liquidation sales or at
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 16 --

6	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 4	Brief Example - Biz Opp
bargain stores, dollar stores, warehouse sales, or the like. I’d probably just
put etcetera. 2015 Stefan said, “Or the like,” but etcetera makes more sense.
(06:36)
But while there are literally millions of retailers who are desperate to find
people to buy their products at a hugely discounted price, there are also tens
of thousands of liquidators who want to buy these same items. It’s called
liquidated buying, and people make billions off this industry. All you need to
do to join these billionaires is act as a broker. You simply find manufacturers
and retailers who have overstocks or too much inventory, connect them
to liquidators and closeout specialists who love buying those products so
they can sell them to their vast networks, and you make a huge profit as the
middle man. You don’t even have to physically touch the products or have one
single employee. Instead, we’ll show you an easy way to ensure retailers and
manufacturers will come to you, and then show you how to find buyers online,
along with each step required to set up the deal and profit from it.
(07:26)
For example, say a large home goods store contacts you with $100,000 worth
of light bulbs they haven’t been able to sell, they need to clear out space for a
new shipment, so they’re willing to sell all of these light bulbs for $30,000. All
you have to do is connect the home goods chain with a discount retailer, and
you net a 20% commission or $6,000 in cold, hard cash. That’s the way this
business works. And literally, all you did was send out a couple of emails and
collect your check.
(07:51)
See, a kind of interesting thing to build a course around. And I think liquidation
buying is a legit thing. I haven’t looked at this in a while, but yeah, kind of
cool. Paradoxical question, what happens to all of the products that retail
and department stores don’t sell? I actually really think that’s a very good
paradoxical question. Or you know, you can put it another way, it would be
something like, “What do outlet stores do with the items they aren’t able to
sell?” ‘Cause that one’s even better because it’s like we know that outlets
get the stuff at the retailer. When a retailer wasn’t able to sell a product in
their main store, they send it to an outlet store. But what happens when the
outlet store isn’t able to sell the product either? And then that’s where we can
answer that with our product, “Well, there’s this whole liquidation business,
and liquidators will buy the for even cheaper, and then they’ll sell it, and you can
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

7	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 4	Brief Example - Biz Opp
even bring in the idea of supply and demand. And basically, if you can connect
the retailers to the liquidators, you can make a middleman commission and
make a lot of money.” Again, a cool concept, in my opinion.
(09:08)
Metaphor, just like a real estate agent makes a fat commission by connecting
a home seller to a home buyer, you can make a fat commission by connecting
the seller of overstocked or excess products to a buyer. But you don’t have
to go out and drive around town to do it, or schedule home tours, or have a
bunch of fancy legal paperwork here. Instead, you simply need your email and
an internet connection. It’s essentially just drawing a straight line between
two dots. They’ll handle all the work, you just build the bridge. Kind of a little
bonus one with, “You just build the bridge.” So, nice, pretty easy to understand
metaphor. It is relevant because we’re talking about connecting two interested
parties and getting a commission. So, everyone kinda knows that’s what a
real estate agent does, and as such, it’s easy to then think about that in terms
of connecting retailers to liquidators as well.
(09:56)
Bold claim, you can make $5,000 per month in extra income by simply acting
as a broker between buyers and sellers. All it takes is an internet connection
and a couple of hours per week of your time. You don’t even need to get on
the phone with people, you don’t have to put out any of your own money, you
don’t even need to have any experience running a business. So that’s the bold
claim. Again, this is the bold claim from the product, right? Because number
three-- they aren’t actually numbered in this one. I’ll make sure the questions
match the version I used right now.
(10:25)
But going back, this one was just the third one, right? That’s about with reading
the letter is that one, which is, again, showing the simple plug and play business
opportunity. And then now, we’re over here to the bold claim, which is like if
they buy your product, what do they get? And I said you can make $5,000 per
month in extra income by simply acting as a broker. And I’ll actually go ahead
and I’ll pause this, and I’ll update the things here for my current brief structure.
That way, it’s still more consistent. Give me one second.
(10:55)
All right, so I updated this to match the questions in the template for the brief

structure as it looks today. And it is cool to think about the fact that--again,

this was during the time where I was able to be quite prolific writing as many

while, and it’s been working quite well.
(11:30)
All right. So, this is the bold claim again about results you’re going to get from
using the product. What is the product, right? This is the part we’re going to
write that out. We’ll show you how to find closeout sales and liquidation items
all across the country and world, and connect them to buyers, wholesalers
who are desperate to get them. You’re filling a great need, and you’re able to
sell millions in inventory without touching it, having a warehouse, worrying
about product management, or anything like that. If you have your computer,
know how to send an email, and have a few hours a week, you can easily make
4K to 10K per month doing this. Inside, you’ll be shown the entire business
model in simple, easy-to-follow steps. You’ll be shown how easy it is to place
free ads on the web that get retailers and manufacturers to come to you with
their overstocked items and goods.
(12:18)
Specifically, you’ll be given 25 websites where you can post your ads for free.
You’re guaranteed to get at least 25 people contacting you per month with
their excess inventory. The next step is for you to find buyers. To help you
do that, we’ll give you a list of more than 150 companies that specialize in
buying overstocked or liquidation items. They are constantly looking for more
goods to sell because that’s how they make money. You’re doing them a huge
service by emailing them or calling them if you want. ‘If you want’ is supposed
to pertain to calling them. Then you’ll be shown the keys to negotiate the deal
as the broker. You’ll see how to make sure you maximize your profits, get the
commission you deserve, and do it without spending hours sweating it out, or
need to hire expensive lawyers. You’ll also discover the 7 questions you need
to ask every retailer or manufacturer that contacts you, and the 12 common
pitfalls you need to avoid so that you’re always coming out on top of the deal.
(13:03)
So, going back, again, we’re going to do a whole module on fascinations, but
these are fascinations right there. They’re kind of curiosity bullets, or things
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

that are designed to increase intrigue, and create intrigue and curiosity, so
that the prospect feels like, “Oh, I want to know the answer to that. I better
either read the sales letter, or in this case, buy the product.” But this is what I

it when I did this brief back in 2014. But as you can see, it’s like this is all stuff
that I can then take and apply to the copy. And that’s what I actually did. When
it came time to introduce the product, perfect, I was able to do that. And I had
a lot of that written already. And I’m sure I probably expanded upon it a bit
more, but it gave me a really great foundation where I didn’t feel overwhelmed
when it came time to write the sales letter.
(13:53)
And here’s my stuff on the background story. You’ll note I kinda did it internal
to myself, and then I kinda shift into what the story can look like. And this is
a made up story that I was doing for this one at the time. But I just want to
go with a pretty classic Biz Opp story here. Basically, Avatar is 55, wants to
retire, but simply can’t afford it. His job is okay, but he’s tired of working or
feeling like a slave, of never having enough time for his family. Feels like life
is passing him by, and worries that he’s going to die one day without ever
having gotten to experience all of the things he dreamed of, from travel to a
nice home. It’s so crazy, it seems like everywhere he turns, these young kids
in their 20s and 30s are in the news making billions of dollars for their simple
websites that don’t even do much. I’ll probably put it like millions.
(14:37)
He dreams about having his own business of doing something similar. But
isn’t he too old? Where would he even start with something like this? Does look
around online some, sees some ‘make money from home’ ads and things like
that, but they’re all scams. He knows that millions of people around the world
are making real money from the internet, but figures they have something he
doesn’t. Then one day, he’s enraged when his wife comes home from running
errands and has spent $300 on new clothes at Marshalls. He tells her, “We
can’t afford to spend money like that.” And she says she’s sorry, but it was
all 50% off and the deal was just too good for her to pass up. That gets him
thinking, even though the store his wife shopped at was already a discount
retailer, they still were having a sale where some of their were discounted
another 50% on top of that.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

10	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 4	Brief Example - Biz Opp
(15:16)
One of the shirts she got, for example, was from a pretty well-known luxury
brand and normally sold for $100, but the discount store was selling it for $50.
And then his wife got another 50% off that, so she only paid $25. It makes
him start wondering, “What happens to all the extra inventory that retailers
aren’t able to sell? Some of it goes to discount stores or outlets right away,
what happens when they don’t sell it either? It never just becomes free, right?
So, do they just destroy it? Or... And that’s just clothing. The same thing has
to happen for everything, from hardware to electronics, even cars.” So, he
starts doing research and discovers that there’s a whole industry out there of
people who buy products that are either overstocked or that haven’t sold at
the regular price and then resell it for cheap to the public.
(15:54)
He doesn’t have the kind of money needed to start his own store, but what he
does realize is that he can act like a middle man, much in the same way a real
estate agent is the middle man in the sale of a home. So, he starts making lists
of all the different liquidators and retailers out there who buy these closeout
items. Then he starts putting ads up saying he can help companies and
manufacturers sell to those liquidators. Before he knows it, he’s acting as a
broker and making a few thousand, and then $40,000 a month. And he doesn’t
have to do anything. Simply send some emails, make a phone call every now
and then, and that’s it. Of course, there’s some trial and error to figure all
this out, which you won’t have to go through since the program shows you
everything. But anyways, incredible changed life, shares with others.

So that’s my story. And frankly, it’s a good story. But you’ll note too, right, in

Though I Never Event Went to College].” That could be a good, decent headline.

But yeah, at least there’s one good one. And ideas for product upsells, these

is email this agreement to both parties to sign and you’re good to go. So, a lot
done for you, which is a good upsell, especially in Biz Opp. If you just bought
like a system or a program, one of the best things that you can sell them is

paste, easy, things like that.”
